title: Debug rerank promotion of a bad candidate

items.text: Trace 22222222-2222-4222-8222-222222222222 shows the correct candidate at retrieval rank 2 and the decoy at retrieval rank 5, then rerank.score promotes the decoy above the correct candidate.
Decoy note: deployment retry discussion shares query terms but belongs to a different project.

answer.content: The wrong result came from rerank.score promoting a cross-project decoy over the correct retrieval candidate.

claims.text: Rerank promoted a cross-project decoy above the correct retrieval candidate.

timeline.summary: The trace captured retrieval ranks and rerank scores for the correct and decoy candidates.

prompt.content: Explain why the wrong note ranked first.

must_include.text: Rerank promoted a cross-project decoy above the correct retrieval candidate.

must_not_include: The correct candidate was missing from retrieval.

required_evidence.quote: rerank.score promotes the decoy above the correct candidate

debuggability.criteria: Uses rerank and replay candidate evidence.

evidence_grounding.criteria: Cites the trace rather than the decoy note.

answer_correctness.criteria: Names rerank promotion as the cause.

hard_fail_rules: unsupported high-confidence claim about a required decision or fact
use of a negative trap marked failure_if_used = true

acceptable_phrases: The fixture does not provide that evidence.

operator_debug.root_cause: The correct item was in the candidate set, but rerank.score elevated a cross-project decoy.
operator_debug.dropped_candidate_visibility: not dropped; visible with lower final rank in Replay Candidates
cli_steps: compare retrieval rank with final rank
